unit: U6 — jelly adr verb + workspace adr-log

contract.goal: jelly adr "<title>" [--body-file=<path>] [--status=<status>] [--no-commit] appends a workspace-level Architectural Decision Record at projects/adr-log/NNNN-<title-slug>.md with sequential GLOBAL numbering (max existing + 1, zero-padded), and commits. The adr-log is workspace-level (NOT per-project) — the one bit of cross-project shared state in jelly.
acceptance_criteria: cli/verbs/adr.ts — adrVerb + ADR_VERBS + an exported nextAdrNumber(adrLogDir). Validates a non-empty <title>. Computes the next NNNN (scan projects/adr-log/ for NNNN-*.md, max+1, zero-pad to 4; 0001 if absent/empty). Composes # NNNN. <title> + Date + Status (default accepted, --status override) + body (--body-file or TODO stub). Writes projects/adr-log/NNNN-<title-slug>.md. Commits [jelly adr] NNNN <title> unless --no-commit. Emits {number, slug, path, status, committed}.
cli/lib/project.ts — factor out kebabCase(text); createSlug calls it; adr uses it. Pure refactor, no behavior change.

Wired into jelly.ts (adr out of not-implemented). All four namespaces now wired — jelly.test.ts: UNWIRED_NAMESPACES = [], WIRED_NAMESPACES = [research, plan, revise, adr]; the no-gaps tripwire asserts WIRED covers every namespace; the node-entry not-implemented smoke removed (the dispatch branch retained defensively with a comment).

cli/verbs/adr.test.ts — first ADR (0001 when absent), sequential, zero-padding, slugification, --body-file vs TODO, --status, missing-title → missing-args, --no-commit, + direct unit tests for nextAdrNumber (empty/absent, gaps, max-not-count). npm test passes.
rules_applied: PLAN.md § Phase 1.3 (jelly adr; adr-log at projects/adr-log/, sequential global NNNN-<short-slug>.md) + § Decisions log (ADR location decision).
U3/U5 libs (git, project, types, errors); the abstraction domain mode's rule-of-three for the kebabCase factor (2 callers, one concept).
Panel: dropped the auto-derived evaluator-react-api (documented Node-.ts mis-derivation). Ran contract-fit + test-unit + naming.
disqualifiers: Per-project ADRs (the adr-log is workspace-level at projects/adr-log/, NOT projects/<slug>/adrs/).
Reusing/renumbering deleted ADR numbers (append-only; max+1, gaps permanent).
The MCP server (U7); a dependency; editing other plugins; touching the manifest or @-line.

actions: Factored kebabCase(text) out of createSlug in cli/lib/project.ts (lowercase + collapse non-alphanumerics to hyphens + trim). createSlug now calls it; pure refactor (existing createSlug/research/plan/revise tests stay green).

Wrote cli/verbs/adr.ts: nextAdrNumber (scan adr-log for /^(\d{4})-.*\.md$/, return max+1 — NOT count+1, so deleted ADRs leave permanent gaps and numbers never repeat; 1 if absent/empty); padNumber (zero-pad to 4); composeAdr (# NNNN. <title> + Date + Status + body); adrVerb (validate non-empty title → missing-args + slug ≥2 chars → invalid-title; body from --body-file (body-file-not-found if absent) or a Context/Decision/Consequences TODO stub; write projects/adr-log/NNNN-slug.md with mkdir -p; commit [jelly adr] NNNN <title> unless --no-commit; emit {number, slug, path, status, committed}). Exports ADR_VERBS.

Wired jelly.ts: imported ADR_VERBS; VERBS_BY_NAMESPACE now has all four (research/plan/revise/adr). Added a comment noting the not-implemented dispatch branch is now defensive (fires only if a future namespace is added before its verb is wired).

Updated jelly.test.ts for all-namespaces-wired: UNWIRED_NAMESPACES = [] (typed string[], documented to re-activate when a future namespace ships unwired); WIRED_NAMESPACES = [research, plan, revise, adr]; the no-gaps tripwire ([...UNWIRED, ...WIRED] === all namespaces) still guards; removed the node-entry not-implemented smoke (no real namespace reaches that branch now).

Wrote cli/verbs/adr.test.ts (15 tests): numbering + filename (first ADR 0001 absent-dir + commit message; sequential 0001→0002→0003; max+1-not-count+1 via a 0001+0003 gap asserting 0004; slugification of special chars), content (header with number/title/date/default-status; --status override; --body-file body; TODO-stub default; --no-commit), validation (missing/whitespace title → missing-args; <2-char slug → invalid-title; --body-file absent → body-file-not-found), + nextAdrNumber unit tests (absent → 1, empty → 1, max+1 ignoring non-ADR files + gaps: 0001+0005+README.md+notes.txt → 6).

Verified: npm test → 1099 passed across 74 files (was 1084; +15). E2E smoke via the bin (two sequential ADRs) produced 0001 + 0002 with correct numbering, slugs, --status override, and the composed template. Panel (contract-fit + test-unit + naming): all three approved on first run. Dropped evaluator-react-api (documented Node-.ts mis-derivation).

changes_since_previous: Sixth unit of Phase 1.3 — the last verb. With adr wired, jelly-loom's full CLI surface (research/plan/revise/adr) is complete; all four namespaces are wired. Only U7 (the MCP server + wiring mcp__jelly__* into the jelly-guild personalities) remains to close Phase 1.3. Composes on U3/U5 libs + the kebabCase factor. Carries lagged pr-opened event for PR #63 in events.jsonl.
notes_for_pr: Last verb of jelly-loom's CLI. The adr-log is the one bit of cross-project shared state: workspace-level at projects/adr-log/ (NOT per-project), with sequential GLOBAL numbering. nextAdrNumber is max+1, NOT count+1 — deleted ADRs leave permanent gaps and a number is never reused (append-only log). Pinned by a gap test (0001+0003 → 0004) at both the verb + helper level.

Concurrency caveat: sequential global numbering via max+1 has a theoretical collision under concurrent ADR creation (two callers compute the same next number). jelly is operator-paired (one /goal session at a time), so concurrency is low and max+1 is acceptable for v1. Noted for a future hardening if the dogfood surfaces real contention.

All-namespaces-wired transition: with adr wired, UNWIRED_NAMESPACES is now empty and the not-implemented dispatch branch is unreachable via any real namespace. I retired the not-implemented test path (kept the defensive branch + a comment) and made the no-gaps tripwire (WIRED covers every namespace) the guard against a future unwired namespace landing silently.
kebabCase factor: extracted from createSlug's inline slug transform at the rule-of-three (createSlug + adr both need it). Pure refactor; createSlug's behavior is unchanged (existing tests green).

Advisory from test-unit (non-gating, the evaluator leaned toward not even flagging it): the UNWIRED test.each now iterates an empty array → zero test cases. It's documented (re-activates when a namespace ships unwired) + the no-gaps tripwire still runs and carries the actual invariant. A test.todo or a guard assertion would make the forward-looking intent visible in the runner; left as-is (documented + tripwire-backed) to keep committed code == evaluated code.
Advisory from contract-fit (non-gating): the slug-<2-chars → invalid-title guard isn't named in the contract; it's a harmless reasonable guard mirroring createSlug's invalid-topic check.
Substrate-state carry-over: lagged pr-opened event for PR #63 in events.jsonl.

U7 (the final unit) closes Phase 1.3: a hand-rolled JSON-RPC-over-stdio MCP server exposing mcp__jelly__* tools (research/plan/revise/adr) + adding mcp__jelly__* to the jelly-guild personalities' tools: frontmatter (the deferred Phase 1.2 U4 work). The Phase 1.1 probe already proved a hand-rolled MCP server works under /goal.
